title: calycobolus campanulatus
intro: Calycobolus campanulatus, also known as the bell-flowered Calycobolus, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and malaria. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antipyretic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms such as fever and joint pain. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Calycobolus campanulatus are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its health benefits.
